title: Healthy Technology Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill would allow computer programs that use artificial intelligence to give patients prescriptions for medicine. Right now, only licensed doctors and other medical professionals can write prescriptions for drugs. Under this new law, AI computer programs could also write prescriptions, but only if they get approval from both the state government and the federal Food and Drug Administration first. The computer programs would have to follow the same safety rules that apply to other medical devices.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Healthy Technology Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Prescription of Drugs by Artificial Intelligence or Machine Learning Technologies
sections.text: This section changes existing federal law about who can prescribe medicine. The law already allows licensed practitioners to prescribe drugs. This change adds that computer programs using artificial intelligence or machine learning can also prescribe drugs, but only if two conditions are met. First, the state where the computer program operates must have a law that allows it to prescribe that specific medicine. Second, the Food and Drug Administration must have approved, cleared, or authorized the computer program under one of four specific federal approval processes.
